description: Teaches the model to compose 2-3 building block patterns into complete game systems. Each example combines timer+counter, branch+state, overlap+destroy+score, etc. This is the critical complexity tier where most real game objects live.

prompts.instruction: Create a bomb that starts a 5 second countdown on overlap. Each second it prints the remaining time. When it reaches zero it prints BOOM, deals damage, and destroys itself
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_TimedBomb
PARENT: Actor

VAR TimeLeft: Int = 5
VAR IsArmed: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=IsArmed]
NODE n3: Not
NODE n4: Branch
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=IsArmed, Value=true]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Bomb armed!"]
NODE n7: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="CountDown", Time=1.0, Looping=true]
NODE n8: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="CountDown"]
NODE n9: GetVar [Variable=TimeLeft]
NODE n10: SubtractFloat [B=1]
NODE n11: SetVar [Variable=TimeLeft]
NODE n12: PrintString
NODE n13: LessThanOrEqual [B=0]
NODE n14: Branch
NODE n15: PrintString [InString="BOOM!"]
NODE n16: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="CountDown"]
NODE n17: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.True -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n12.Execute
EXEC n12.Then -> n14.Execute
EXEC n14.True -> n15.Execute
EXEC n15.Then -> n16.Execute
EXEC n16.Then -> n17.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Condition [Bool]
DATA n9.Value -> n10.A [Int]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n11.Value [Int]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n12.InString [String]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n13.A [Int]
DATA n13.ReturnValue -> n14.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a wave spawner. On BeginPlay start a 15 second looping timer. Each time it fires, increment WaveNumber, print Wave X, and check if WaveNumber exceeds 10. If so, print All Waves Complete and clear the timer
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_WaveSpawner
PARENT: Actor

VAR WaveNumber: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="SpawnWave", Time=15.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="SpawnWave"]
NODE n4: GetVar [Variable=WaveNumber]
NODE n5: AddFloat [B=1]
NODE n6: SetVar [Variable=WaveNumber]
NODE n7: PrintString
NODE n8: GreaterThan [B=10]
NODE n9: Branch
NODE n10: PrintString [InString="All Waves Complete!"]
NODE n11: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="SpawnWave"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.True -> n10.Execute
EXEC n10.Then -> n11.Execute

DATA n4.Value -> n5.A [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Value [Int]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n7.InString [String]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n8.A [Int]
DATA n8.ReturnValue -> n9.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a gem pickup with a PointValue variable defaulting to 25. On overlap, add PointValue to a Score variable, print the new score, play a sound, and destroy itself
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_GemPickup
PARENT: Actor

VAR PointValue: Int = 25
VAR Score: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n3: GetVar [Variable=PointValue]
NODE n4: AddFloat
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n6: PrintString
NODE n7: PlaySound2D
NODE n8: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n4.A [Int]
DATA n3.Value -> n4.B [Int]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.Value [Int]
DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n6.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create a pressure plate with IsPressed boolean and a LinkedDoor actor reference. On BeginOverlap set IsPressed to true and print Door Opening. On EndOverlap set IsPressed to false and print Door Closing
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_PressurePlate
PARENT: Actor

VAR IsPressed: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: SetVar [Variable=IsPressed, Value=true]
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Door Opening"]
NODE n4: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n5: SetVar [Variable=IsPressed, Value=false]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Door Closing"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create a destructible actor with Health=100. On AnyDamage subtract the damage from Health. If Health drops to zero or below, print Destroyed, play a sound, and destroy. Otherwise print the remaining health
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Destructible
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n3: SubtractFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n5: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Destroyed!"]
NODE n8: PlaySound2D
NODE n9: DestroyActor
NODE n10: PrintString

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute
EXEC n6.False -> n10.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.Damage -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n10.InString [String]

prompts.instruction: Create an enemy with Health=100, a 0.5 second looping timer that checks distance to the player. When within 500 units, apply 10 damage via print. When Health reaches zero from AnyDamage, destroy
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_MeleeEnemy
PARENT: Actor

VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="CheckRange", Time=0.5, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="CheckRange"]
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: LessThan [B=500.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Attacking! -10 HP"]
NODE n8: Event_AnyDamage
NODE n9: GetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n10: SubtractFloat
NODE n11: SetVar [Variable=Health]
NODE n12: LessThanOrEqual [B=0.0]
NODE n13: Branch
NODE n14: DestroyActor

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n13.Execute
EXEC n13.True -> n14.Execute

DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]
DATA n9.Value -> n10.A [Float]
DATA n8.Damage -> n10.B [Float]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n11.Value [Float]
DATA n10.ReturnValue -> n12.A [Float]
DATA n12.ReturnValue -> n13.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a locked door with HasKey boolean. On overlap if HasKey is true, print Door Unlocked and destroy the door. If HasKey is false, print You need a key. Also has a custom event CollectKey that sets HasKey to true and prints Key Collected
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_LockedDoor
PARENT: Actor

VAR HasKey: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=HasKey]
NODE n3: Branch
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Door Unlocked!"]
NODE n5: DestroyActor
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="You need a key"]
NODE n7: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="CollectKey"]
NODE n8: SetVar [Variable=HasKey, Value=true]
NODE n9: PrintString [InString="Key Collected!"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.True -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n3.False -> n6.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a shop terminal. On BeginOverlap create a WBP_Shop widget, add it to viewport, and print Shop Opened. On EndOverlap remove the widget from parent and print Shop Closed
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ShopTerminal
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: CreateWidget [WidgetClass=WBP_Shop]
NODE n3: AddToViewport
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Shop Opened"]
NODE n5: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n6: RemoveFromParent
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Shop Closed"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.Target [Widget]
DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n6.Target [Widget]

prompts.instruction: Create a checkpoint. On overlap save the player's location to SavedPosition and print Checkpoint. Has a custom event Respawn that teleports the player back to SavedPosition, resets Health to 100, and prints Respawned
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Checkpoint
PARENT: Actor

VAR SavedPosition: Vector
VAR Health: Float = 100.0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetActorLocation
NODE n3: SetVar [Variable=SavedPosition]
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Checkpoint!"]
NODE n5: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="Respawn"]
NODE n6: GetVar [Variable=SavedPosition]
NODE n7: SetActorLocation
NODE n8: SetVar [Variable=Health, Value=100.0]
NODE n9: PrintString [InString="Respawned!"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

DATA n2.ReturnValue -> n3.Value [Vector]
DATA n6.Value -> n7.NewLocation [Vector]

prompts.instruction: Create a day night controller. Timer variable increments by DeltaSeconds on Tick. When Timer exceeds 60, reset Timer to 0, toggle IsNight using FlipFlop, and print either Night Falls or Dawn Breaks
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_DayNight
PARENT: Actor

VAR Timer: Float = 0.0
VAR IsNight: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_Tick
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Timer]
NODE n3: AddFloat
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Timer]
NODE n5: GreaterThan [B=60.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: SetVar [Variable=Timer, Value=0.0]
NODE n8: FlipFlop
NODE n9: SetVar [Variable=IsNight, Value=true]
NODE n10: PrintString [InString="Night Falls"]
NODE n11: SetVar [Variable=IsNight, Value=false]
NODE n12: PrintString [InString="Dawn Breaks"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.A -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute
EXEC n8.B -> n11.Execute
EXEC n11.Then -> n12.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Float]
DATA n1.DeltaSeconds -> n3.B [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Float]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a turret with a 0.5 second range check timer. When the player is within 1000 units, print Firing and deal damage. When out of range, print Scanning. Track InRange boolean
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Turret
PARENT: Actor

VAR InRange: Bool = false

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="ScanTarget", Time=0.5, Looping=true]
NODE n3: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="ScanTarget"]
NODE n4: GetDistanceTo
NODE n5: LessThan [B=1000.0]
NODE n6: Branch
NODE n7: SetVar [Variable=InRange, Value=true]
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="Firing!"]
NODE n9: SetVar [Variable=InRange, Value=false]
NODE n10: PrintString [InString="Scanning..."]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.True -> n7.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n6.False -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute

DATA n4.ReturnValue -> n5.A [Float]
DATA n5.ReturnValue -> n6.Condition [Bool]

prompts.instruction: Create a score manager with Score and HighScore. Custom event AddPoints adds 10 to Score. After adding, if Score exceeds HighScore, set HighScore to Score and print New High Score. Always print current Score
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_ScoreManager
PARENT: Actor

VAR Score: Int = 0
VAR HighScore: Int = 0

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="AddPoints"]
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n3: AddFloat [B=10]
NODE n4: SetVar [Variable=Score]
NODE n5: PrintString
NODE n6: GetVar [Variable=HighScore]
NODE n7: GreaterThan
NODE n8: Branch
NODE n9: SetVar [Variable=HighScore]
NODE n10: PrintString [InString="New High Score!"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.True -> n9.Execute
EXEC n9.Then -> n10.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.A [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n4.Value [Int]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n5.InString [String]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n7.A [Int]
DATA n6.Value -> n7.B [Int]
DATA n7.ReturnValue -> n8.Condition [Bool]
DATA n3.ReturnValue -> n9.Value [Int]

prompts.instruction: Create a moving platform. On BeginPlay print Platform Active. Use a looping timer that alternates direction with FlipFlop. On A, move up 300 units and print Going Up. On B, move down 300 units and print Going Down
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_MovingPlatform
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_BeginPlay
NODE n2: PrintString [InString="Platform Active"]
NODE n3: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="MovePlatform", Time=3.0, Looping=true]
NODE n4: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="MovePlatform"]
NODE n5: FlipFlop
NODE n6: AddActorLocalOffset [DeltaLocation={X=0,Y=0,Z=300}]
NODE n7: PrintString [InString="Going Up"]
NODE n8: AddActorLocalOffset [DeltaLocation={X=0,Y=0,Z=-300}]
NODE n9: PrintString [InString="Going Down"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.A -> n6.Execute
EXEC n6.Then -> n7.Execute
EXEC n5.B -> n8.Execute
EXEC n8.Then -> n9.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create a lava damage zone. On BeginOverlap start a 1 second looping damage timer and print Burning. On EndOverlap clear the timer and print Safe. The timer event subtracts 10 from the player's health each tick and prints the damage
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_LavaDamage
PARENT: Actor

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: SetTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="DealDamage", Time=1.0, Looping=true]
NODE n3: PrintString [InString="Burning!"]
NODE n4: Event_ActorEndOverlap
NODE n5: ClearTimerByFunctionName [FunctionName="DealDamage"]
NODE n6: PrintString [InString="Safe"]
NODE n7: Event_CustomEvent [EventName="DealDamage"]
NODE n8: PrintString [InString="-10 HP"]

EXEC n1.Then -> n2.Execute
EXEC n2.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute
EXEC n5.Then -> n6.Execute
EXEC n7.Then -> n8.Execute

prompts.instruction: Create a teleporter with a TargetLocation vector variable. On overlap, get the player reference from the overlap event, set the player's location to TargetLocation, print Teleported, and play a sound
prompts.expected_dsl:
BLUEPRINT: BP_Teleporter
PARENT: Actor

VAR TargetLocation: Vector

GRAPH: EventGraph

NODE n1: Event_ActorBeginOverlap
NODE n2: GetVar [Variable=TargetLocation]
NODE n3: SetActorLocation
NODE n4: PrintString [InString="Teleported!"]
NODE n5: PlaySound2D

EXEC n1.Then -> n3.Execute
EXEC n3.Then -> n4.Execute
EXEC n4.Then -> n5.Execute

DATA n2.Value -> n3.NewLocation [Vector]
DATA n1.OtherActor -> n3.Target [Actor]
